div.head:
No. 895.
Mr. Roosevelt to Mr. Bayard.

opener.dateline:
Legation of the United States,
The Hague, October 20, 1888. (Received October 30.)

div.p: Sir: In reference to the case of Mr. Hector, who complained of the refusal of the railways to carry hog products which came from America, mentioned in my dispatch No. 23, of September 28, I have reason to believe that he had not complied with the requisite forms, and having so informed him, have heard nothing further in the matter, and shall do nothing further at present.
